text.title: Edge and CDN Strategy Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: User A receives User B's personalised response from cache.
antipattern.reason: Cache key missed the auth boundary (cookie / Authorization header).
antipattern.note: Fix: Add auth-bearing header to cache key. Audit cached responses by sampling. Purge cache for the affected route.

antipattern.description: Cache flush triggers origin overload; 5xx for 10 minutes.
antipattern.reason: No shielding; every edge fetches from origin in parallel.
antipattern.note: Fix: Enable origin shielding. Configure stale-while-revalidate to serve stale during refresh.

antipattern.description: Auth bug fix lands in edge code but not origin; users locked out intermittently.
antipattern.note: Fix: Pick one: edge auth with signed-identity handoff to origin, or pass-through edge with origin auth. Delete the duplicate.

antipattern.description: Cloudflare incident; site unreachable; team scrambles to find a way to bypass.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add origin.acme.com DNS record. Document bypass procedure in DR runbook. Test quarterly.

antipattern.description: Monthly edge bill 5x expected; nobody knew.
antipattern.reason: No per-request cost budget; a regex catastrophic loop ran for weeks.
antipattern.note: Fix: Set per-request budget. Alert at 1.5x budget. Audit top routes by cost weekly.
